certification_guidelines.title: Handloom Mark Guidelines for Co-operative Societies
certification_guidelines.target_applicant: co-operative societies engaged in handloom weaving and marketing
certification_guidelines.content_html:
Handloom Mark Guidelines for Co-operative Societies

These guidelines outline eligibility, documentation, and fee structure for co-operative societies applying for the Handloom Mark.

Eligibility

  • Registered handloom co-operative societies with at least 25 active weaver members.
  • Valid registration under state co-operative societies act.

Application Process

  1. Fill the prescribed application form online or offline.
  2. Attach registration certificate, list of members, and production details.
  3. Pay the applicable application fee as per the fee structure table below.

Fee Structure

Refer to the Application Fees table on this page for detailed fee slabs based on turnover and production volume.

certification_guidelines.title: Handloom Mark Guidelines for Individual Weavers
certification_guidelines.target_applicant: individual handloom weavers and master weavers
certification_guidelines.content_html:
Handloom Mark Guidelines for Individual Weavers

These guidelines explain how individual weavers can obtain the Handloom Mark for their products.

Quality Mark Standards for Handloom Products

Minimum quality parameters for obtaining the Quality Mark certification.

events.title: National Handloom Expo & Exhibition – Delhi 2025
events.description: A national-level handloom exhibition showcasing traditional and contemporary handloom products from across India, with dedicated buyer-seller meets and design workshops.
events.address: Hall 7, Pragati Maidan, Mathura Road, New Delhi - 110001
events.stall_booking_details: Stall booking is available for co-operatives, individual weavers and producer companies. Early-bird concession of 10% till 31 August 2025.

events.title: Delhi Handloom & Crafts Mela 2025
events.description: A cultural crafts mela with handloom, handicrafts, folk performances and food courts.
events.address: Dilli Haat, INA, Sri Aurobindo Marg, New Delhi - 110023

events.title: South India Handloom Fair 2025
events.description: Regional handloom fair for southern states with buyer-seller meets.
events.stall_booking_details: Limited stalls available for micro-enterprises and individual weavers.

events.title: National Conference on Sustainable Handloom
events.description: Experts, policy makers and weaver representatives discuss sustainability and green practices in handloom.

events.title: Winter Handloom Expo – Delhi 2025
events.description: Completed winter season handloom expo in Delhi.

events.title: Design Innovation Workshop for Odisha Handloom Clusters
events.description: Two-day in-person workshop on contemporary design trends for Odisha clusters.

events.title: Online Webinar: Digital Marketing for Handloom Exports
events.description: A 2-hour online webinar focusing on digital marketing strategies for promoting handloom products in export markets.

events.title: Chennai Handloom Festival 2024
events.description: State-level handloom festival with live demonstrations and cultural performances.
events.stall_booking_details: Stalls allotted to registered co-operatives and producer companies.

schemes.name: Modern Looms Upgradation Assistance for Individual Weavers
schemes.description: Financial assistance to individual handloom weavers for replacement and upgradation of looms and accessories to improve productivity and quality.
schemes.objectives: To facilitate modernization of handlooms and adoption of improved technologies by individual weavers.
schemes.eligibility: Individual handloom weavers with at least 3 years of weaving experience and valid Weaver ID card.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy up to 50% of the cost of new loom and accessories subject to scheme ceiling.
schemes.application_process: Applications to be submitted online through the state handloom portal along with required documents. Screening will be done by district-level committee.

schemes.name: Digital Marketing Support for Individual Handloom Weavers
schemes.description: Support to individual weavers for onboarding to e-commerce platforms and for digital marketing campaigns.
schemes.objectives: To enhance online presence and digital sales of handloom products produced by individual weavers.
schemes.eligibility: Individual weavers registered with Handloom Mark or state handloom department; basic digital literacy preferred.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy up to 40% on onboarding and service charges of approved e-commerce platforms plus fixed support for product photography.
required_documents: Proof of Handloom Mark or registration
schemes.application_process: Submit online application with platform preference. On approval, the department will coordinate onboarding and reimburse eligible expenses.

schemes.name: Raw Material (Yarn & Dyes) Supply and Subsidy Scheme for Individual Weavers
schemes.description: Scheme to provide timely supply of yarn, dyes and chemicals to individual handloom weavers at subsidised rates through yarn depots and mobile vans.
schemes.objectives: To ensure continuous and affordable supply of quality yarn and dyes to individual weavers and reduce production costs.
schemes.eligibility: Individual weavers registered with state handloom department or co-operative societies; priority for BPL and SC/ST weavers.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy up to 35% on basic yarn cost within prescribed quantity limits per month.
required_documents: Recommendation from Weavers Service Centre or co-operative society
schemes.application_process: Weavers may register at the nearest yarn depot or online portal. After approval, purchases from designated depots will automatically reflect the applicable subsidy.

schemes.name: Health Insurance Scheme for Handloom Weavers
schemes.description: Health insurance coverage for individual handloom weavers and their families.
schemes.objectives: To provide affordable health insurance benefits to weavers.
schemes.eligibility: All registered handloom weavers below a specified income threshold.
schemes.benefits: Premium subsidy up to 80% for family floater policy.
schemes.application_process: Applications were accepted through implementing insurance companies and state handloom departments.

schemes.name: Cluster Infrastructure Support Scheme for Handloom Co-operatives
schemes.description: Infrastructure support for handloom co-operative societies operating in identified clusters for common facility centres, dye houses and design studios.
schemes.objectives: To strengthen physical infrastructure for handloom production and finishing in major clusters.
schemes.eligibility: Registered handloom co-operative societies in notified clusters with minimum 50 active weaver members.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy up to 60% of project cost subject to cluster-wise ceiling.
required_documents: Audited financial statements for last 3 years
schemes.application_process: Proposals to be submitted through the state handloom directorate. Approved proposals will be forwarded to the Ministry.

schemes.name: Marketing Support Scheme for Handloom Co-operatives
schemes.description: Assistance to handloom co-operative societies for participation in domestic and international marketing events.
schemes.objectives: To expand market access and branding opportunities for handloom co-operative societies.
schemes.eligibility: All registered handloom co-operatives with audited accounts for last 2 years.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy on stall rentals, transportation and publicity expenses.
schemes.application_process: Societies may apply through the online portal at least 30 days prior to the event.

schemes.name: Working Capital Credit Support for Handloom Producer Companies
schemes.description: Interest subvention and credit guarantee support for handloom producer companies to meet working capital needs.
schemes.objectives: To improve access to affordable working capital for handloom producer companies.
schemes.eligibility: Handloom producer companies registered under Companies Act with minimum paid-up capital and turnover thresholds.
schemes.benefits: Interest subvention up to 5% and partial credit guarantee fee subsidy.
schemes.application_process: Eligible entities can apply online with details of sanctioned credit limit.

schemes.name: Export Promotion Scheme for Handloom State Agencies
schemes.description: Support to state handloom agencies for participation in international trade fairs and branding initiatives.
schemes.objectives: To enhance overseas market presence of Indian handloom products through state agencies.
schemes.eligibility: State-level handloom corporations and apex societies.
schemes.benefits: Support for stall rentals, shipment of samples and promotional materials.
schemes.application_process: State agencies may submit proposals to the Ministry through the online portal.

schemes.name: Skill Upgradation & Capacity Building Scheme
schemes.description: Skill development and capacity building for weavers through structured training programmes.
schemes.objectives: To enhance the skills of existing and new weavers through training on design, quality and technology.
schemes.eligibility: Training institutions, Weavers Service Centres and implementing agencies empanelled by the Ministry.
schemes.benefits: Full or partial reimbursement of training costs and stipends for trainees.
schemes.application_process: Institutions submit training proposals for approval; post-approval funds are released as per milestones.

statistic_indicators.name: Handloom export value
statistic_indicators.description: Annual value of exports of handloom products, including fabrics, sarees, made-ups and furnishings.

statistic_indicators.name: Domestic handloom sales
statistic_indicators.description: Estimated value of domestic sales of handloom products across all channels.

statistic_indicators.name: Number of handloom clusters
statistic_indicators.description: Total number of officially recognised handloom clusters by state and year.

application_fee_categories.applicant_category: Micro co-operative societies (annual turnover up to ₹25 lakh)
application_fee_categories.notes: Concessional fee applicable to village-level co-operatives with up to 50 active weaver members.

application_fee_categories.applicant_category: Small co-operative societies (turnover above ₹25 lakh and up to ₹1 crore)
application_fee_categories.notes: Includes primary weavers’ co-operatives operating at block or taluk level.

application_fee_categories.applicant_category: Medium co-operative societies (turnover above ₹1 crore and up to ₹5 crore)
application_fee_categories.notes: Applicable to district-level co-operatives with own retail outlets.

application_fee_categories.applicant_category: Large co-operative federations (turnover above ₹5 crore and up to ₹20 crore)
application_fee_categories.notes: State-level federations and apex co-operatives not engaged in direct exports.
Highest slab; applicable to co-operatives directly exporting handloom products. Includes up to two product categories under one application.
Non-refundable processing fee, payable in addition to the applicable application fee slab.
Concessional fee for first-time Handloom Mark registration for individual weavers.

application_fee_categories.applicant_category: Individual weavers  renewal (on or before due date)
application_fee_categories.notes: Applicable for timely renewal of Handloom Mark license; grace period of 30 days.

application_fee_categories.applicant_category: Individual weavers  renewal with late fee (after due date)
application_fee_categories.notes: Includes late fee component; renewal allowed up to 6 months after expiry.
Core applicant group responsible for GI ownership and maintenance for registered handloom sarees.
Optional co-applicant fee when state agency is listed as joint proprietor of the GI.
Covers inspection and certification for up to 50 SKUs of handloom products.

application_fee_categories.applicant_category: Export houses and large producer companies
application_fee_categories.notes: Includes periodic surveillance audits and use of quality mark in international markets.

programs.title: Advanced Dobby Design Workshop  Mysuru
programs.address: Weavers Service Centre Campus, Bannimantap, Mysuru  570015
programs.description: Hands-on advanced dobby design and weaving workshop for experienced handloom weavers from Karnataka clusters.
programs.objectives: To enhance dobby design capabilities, improve production efficiency and introduce contemporary patterns.
programs.eligibility: Practicing handloom weavers from Karnataka with minimum 3 years experience, nominated by cluster-level institutions.

programs.title: Intensive Jacquard Weaving & Design Workshop  Bengaluru
programs.address: Handloom Training Centre, Magadi Road, Bengaluru  560023
programs.description: A 21-day residential skill upgradation workshop on jacquard weaving, colour combinations, and market-oriented design development for weavers from urban and semi-urban clusters.
programs.objectives: To build capacity of weavers in handling jacquard looms, translating design graphs to fabric, and developing contemporary designs suitable for domestic and export markets.
programs.eligibility: Handloom weavers from recognised clusters in Karnataka having basic jacquard experience; preference to youth and women weavers.
programs.contact_name: Program Officer (Training), Directorate of Handlooms & Textiles

programs.title: Natural Dyeing Techniques for Handloom Weavers  Dharwad
programs.address: Near Textile Park, Hubballi-Dharwad Road, Dharwad  580001
programs.description: Skill development on preparation and application of natural dyes for cotton and silk handloom fabrics, focusing on eco-friendly practices.
programs.objectives: To promote safe and eco-friendly dyeing methods and diversify the colour palette of cluster products.
programs.eligibility: Registered handloom weavers and dyers from Karnataka; basic knowledge of dyeing preferred.

programs.title: Handloom Entrepreneurship & Business Management Bootcamp  Bengaluru
programs.description: Non-residential business management bootcamp for weavers and young entrepreneurs focusing on costing, pricing and market linkages.
programs.objectives: To build managerial and entrepreneurial capacities for sustainable handloom enterprises.
programs.eligibility: Weavers, co-operative managers and youth from handloom families in Karnataka.

programs.title: Basic Weaving Refresher Course  Ballari
programs.description: Short refresher course for weavers on loom maintenance and basic weave structures.
programs.objectives: To reduce loom downtime and improve quality through better maintenance practices.

programs.title: Design Innovation Workshop for Odisha Handloom Clusters  Bhubaneswar
programs.description: Two-day design innovation program for representatives from major Odisha handloom clusters focusing on colour, motifs and product mix.
programs.objectives: To encourage contemporary design development while retaining traditional identity of Odisha handlooms.
programs.eligibility: Cluster-level designers, master weavers and co-operative representatives nominated by district officials.

programs.title: Cluster Management & Marketing for Handloom Leaders  Cuttack
programs.venue_name: State Institute for Handloom Management, Cuttack
programs.description: Residential training for cluster leaders on planning, financial management and marketing of handloom products.
programs.objectives: To strengthen institutional capacities of cluster-level organisations for sustainable development.
programs.eligibility: Cluster development executives, co-operative managers and producer company staff from recognised handloom clusters.
programs.contact_name: Director, State Institute for Handloom Management

programs.title: Digital Marketing for Handloom Exports
programs.description: A 2-hour online webinar focusing on digital marketing strategies, social media campaigns and online branding for promoting handloom products in export markets.
programs.objectives: To familiarise weavers and small enterprises with low-cost digital marketing tools and techniques suited for international buyers.
programs.eligibility: Individual weavers, co-operative representatives and handloom entrepreneurs interested in export markets.

programs.title: Selling Handloom Products on E-commerce Marketplaces
programs.description: Practical guidance on onboarding handloom businesses to major e-commerce marketplaces, managing catalogues and handling logistics.
programs.objectives: To enable weavers and small enterprises to effectively list and sell products on e-commerce platforms and handle online customer orders.
programs.eligibility: Weavers, artisans, producer companies and co-operative staff planning to sell online.

programs.title: Branding & Social Media Strategy for Handloom Businesses
programs.description: Webinar on building a distinct brand identity and using social media platforms to increase visibility and sales of handloom products.
programs.objectives: To help participants create simple branding plans, choose appropriate social media channels and measure engagement.
programs.eligibility: Weavers, entrepreneurs, cluster leaders and marketing staff from co-operatives and producer companies.

programs.title: Export Readiness for Handloom Co-operatives and Producer Companies
programs.description: Session on export procedures, documentation, buyer communication and compliance requirements specific to handloom exports.
programs.objectives: To prepare co-operatives and producer companies for engaging with international buyers and participating in export promotion schemes.
programs.eligibility: Representatives of handloom co-operatives, producer companies and state handloom agencies.

programs.title: Financial Management & Credit Access for Handloom Weavers
programs.description: Webinar covering basics of bookkeeping, working capital planning and accessing government credit support schemes for handloom weavers.
programs.objectives: To improve financial literacy among weavers and facilitate better utilisation of credit-linked schemes.
programs.eligibility: Weavers, artisans, producer groups and cluster facilitators.
